description:

Title: Language models, like humans, show content effects on reasoning tasks
Journal: PNAS Nexus
Year: 2024
Volume: 3
reason: The paper is confirmed by multiple Perplexity searches with matching title, authors, year, journal (PNAS Nexus), volume, issue, article ID, and DOI (10.1093/pnasnexus/pgae233). Discrepancy in author order is common across databases; absence from Semantic Scholar does not invalidate publication.
prompt_variant: You are a critical academic research verifier. Your task: determine if a bibliog...
_search_results:
This paper **exists** and has been published. Here are the exact details:

**Title:** Language models, like humans, show content effects on reasoning tasks[3]

**Authors:** Andrew K Lampinen, Ishita Dasgupta, Stephanie C Y Chan, Hannah R Sheahan, Antonia Creswell, Dharshan Kumaran, James L McClelland, Felix Hill[3]

**Venue:** PNAS Nexus[3]

**Year:** 2024[3]

**Publication Date:** July 16, 2024[3]

**Volume/Issue:** 3(7)[3]

**Article Number:** pgae233[3]

**DOI:** 10.1093/pnasnexus/pgae233[3]

The paper investigates whether large language models exhibit the same **content effects** on reasoning tasks as humans do—that is, whether both humans and models perform better on logical reasoning problems when the semantic content supports correct inferences.[3] The research examines three logical reasoning tasks: natural language inference, syllogism validity judgments, and the Wason selection task.[3]
Semantic Scholar: no matching paper found.

**Yes, the paper exists.**

**Exact title:** Language models, like humans, show content effects on reasoning tasks[1][3][4][5][6].

**Authors:** Andrew K Lampinen, Ishita Dasgupta, Stephanie C Y Chan, Hannah R Sheahan, Antonia Creswell, Dharshan Kumaran, James L McClelland, Felix Hill[5][6].

**Venue:** PNAS Nexus[1][2][3][4][5][6].

**Year:** 2024 (published 16 July 2024; volume 3, issue 7)[4][5][6].

**DOI:** 10.1093/pnasnexus/pgae233[1][2][3][4][5][6].

**Yes, the article exists.**

**Exact title:** Language models, like humans, show content effects on reasoning tasks  
**Authors:** Andrew K Lampinen, Ishita Dasgupta, Stephanie C Y Chan, Hannah R Sheahan, Antonia Creswell, Dharshan Kumaran, James L McClelland, Felix Hill[1][4][5]  
**Venue:** PNAS Nexus  
**Year:** 2024 (published 16 July 2024)[1][5]  
**Volume/Issue/Page:** Volume 3, Issue 7, pgae233[1][4][5]  
**DOI:** https://doi.org/10.1093/pnasnexus/pgae233[1][4][5]  

This open-access article appears in the July 2024 issue (Volume 3, Issue 7) of PNAS Nexus, published by Oxford Academic. It investigates how language models exhibit content biases in abstract reasoning tasks, similar to humans, with validation on human participants and models like Gemini and Gemma[5][1]. The query's "pgae233" matches the article identifier exactly, confirming the reference[1][5].
